#--------------------
# Title
#   Study_Name: Red Sea 150KYr Relative Sea Level Reconstruction
#--------------------
# Investigators
#   Investigators: Grant, K.M.(https://orcid.org/0000-0003-4299-5504); Rohling, E.J.(https://orcid.org/0000-0001-5349-2158); Bar-Matthews, M.(https://orcid.org/0000-0001-8373-5341); Ayalon, A.; Medina-Elizalde, M.(https://orcid.org/0000-0002-0186-9096); Bronk Ramsey, C.(https://orcid.org/0000-0002-8641-9309); Satow, C.(https://orcid.org/0000-0001-7697-0562); Roberts, A.P.(https://orcid.org/0000-0003-0566-8117)
#--------------------
# Description_Notes_and_Keywords
#   Description: All datapoints in the Red Sea sea-level record (RSL) on its new chronology, including fully-propagated chronological uncertainties and RSL smoothed with a 1 kyr moving Gaussian filter
#--------------------
# Publication
#   Authors: K.M. Grant, E.J. Rohling, M. Bar-Matthews, A. Ayalon, M. Medina-Elizalde, C. Bronk Ramsey, C. Satow and A.P. Roberts
#   Journal_Name: Nature
#   Published_Title: Rapid coupling between ice volume and polar temperature over the past 150,000  years
#   Published_Date_or_Year: 2012
#   Volume: 491
#   Issue: 7246
#   Pages: 744-747
#   Report_Number: 
#   DOI: 10.1038/nature11593
#   Full_Citation:
#   Abstract: Current global warming necessitates a detailed understanding of the relationships between climate and global ice volume. Highly resolved and continuous sea-level records are essential for quantifying ice-volume changes. However, an unbiased study of the timing of past ice-volume changes, relative to polar climate change, has so far been impossible because available sea-level records either were dated by using orbital tuning or ice-core timescales, or were discontinuous in time. Here we present an independent dating of a continuous, high-resolution sea-level record in millennial-scale detail throughout the past 150,000 years. We find that the timing of ice-volume fluctuations agrees well with that of variations in Antarctic climate and especially Greenland climate. Amplitudes of ice-volume fluctuations more closely match Antarctic (rather than Greenland) climate changes. Polar climate and ice-volume changes, and their rates of change, are found to covary within centennial response times. Finally, rates of sea-level rise reached at least 1.2 m per century during all major episodes of ice-volume reduction.
#--------------------
# Funding_Agency
#   Funding_Agency_Name: UK Natural Environment Research Council (NERC)
#   Grant: NE/H004424/1, NE/E01531X/1, NE/I009906/1
#--------------------
# Funding_Agency
#   Funding_Agency_Name: Australian Postgraduate Award
#   Grant: FL120100050
#--------------------
# Funding_Agency
#   Funding_Agency_Name: Royal Society
#   Grant: Wolfson Research Merit Award
#--------------------
# Site_Information
#   Site_Name: Soreq Cave
#   Location: Israel
#   Northernmost_Latitude: 31.8
#   Southernmost_Latitude: 31.8
#   Easternmost_Longitude: 35.0
#   Westernmost_Longitude: 35.0
#   Elevation_m: 400
#--------------------
# Data_Collection
#   Collection_Name: Soreq2012
#   First_Year: 161027
#   Last_Year: 0
#   Time_Unit: cal yr BP
#   Core_Length_m:
#   Parameter_Keywords: oxygen isotopes
#   Notes: Soreq Cave U-Th dating contains analytical details of new MC-ICP-MS U/Th ages from Soreq Cave speleothems (see also Grant et al. 2012 "Methods Summary" and  Section 2 of the Supplementary Information which accompanies the study). Soreq Cave d18O contains the Soreq d18O record, the MC-ICP-MS U/Th datings on which it is based, and previous TIMS datings. The Bayesian-modelled MC-ICP-MS ages and uncertainties, and the Soreq d18O record rescaled to these modelled ages, are also shown.
#--------------------
# Chronology_Information
# Chronology:
# 	Dating results for Soreq cave speleothems. Methods are detailed in section 2 of the Supplementary Information which accompanies this study.
#
# 	Columns A-C: Specimen type, speleothem name, and individual sample code
# 	Columns D-E: 238U concentration in sample (ppm) with 2sigma uncertainty
# 	Columns G-N: Isotope activity ratios, with 2 sigma uncertainties
# 	Columns P-Q: U/Th age in kyr, not corrected for detrital 230Th, with 2 sigma uncertainties
# 	Column R: U/Th age in kyr, after correction for detrital 230Th if (230Th/232Th)A < 160 (see section 2, Supplementary Information).
#
# 	Specimen	Speleothem	Sample	[238U]	±2s		(234U/238U)A	±2s		(230Th/232Th)A	±2s		(230Th/234U)A	±2s		Ageuncorr	±2s	Agecorr
#
# 	stalagmite	SO-38	SO-38-C1	1.0383	0.0005		1.0256	0.0008		15.12	0.71		0.0010	0.0000		0.11	0.01	0.08
# 	stalagmite	SO-38	SO-38-C2	1.0383	0.0005		1.0256	0.0008		15.61	0.57		0.0011	0.0000		0.12	0.01	0.09
# 	stalagmite	SO-38	SO-38-C3	1.0552	0.0009		1.0293	0.0008		13.82	0.38		0.0022	0.0001		0.24	0.01	0.19
#

# Variables
# PaST_Thesaurus_Download_Resource: https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/access/paleo-search/skos/past-thesaurus.rdf
#   PaST_Thesaurus_Download_Description: Paleoenvironmental Standard Terms (PaST) Thesaurus terms, definitions, and relationships in SKOS format.
#
# Data variables follow that are preceded by '##' in columns one and two.
# Variables list, one per line, shortname-tab-var components: what, material, error, units, seasonality, data type, detail, method, C or N for Character or Numeric data)
#
## age_calkaBP	age,,,calendar kiloyear before present,,speleothems,,,N,based on MC-ICP-MS datings
## d18OcarbVPDB	delta 18O,calcium carbonate,,per mil VPDB,,speleothems,,isotope ratio mass spectrometry,N,prior to Bayesian modelling
## age_calkaBP_TIMS	age,,,calendar kiloyear before present,,speleothems,,,N,from previous TIMS dating
## age_calkaBP_TIMSerr	age,,two standard deviations,calendar kiloyear before present,,speleothems,,,N,from previous TIMS dating
## age_calkaBP_ICPMS	age,,,calendar kiloyear before present,,speleothems,,,N,from MC-ICP-MS
## age_calkaBP_ICPMSerr	age,,two standard deviations,calendar kiloyear before present,,speleothems,,,N,from MC-ICP-MS
## age_calkaBP_Bayes	age,,,calendar kiloyear before present,,speleothems,corrected,,N,Bayesian-modelled MC-ICP-MS ages
## age_calkaBP_Bayeserr	age,,two standard deviations,calendar kiloyear before present,,speleothems,corrected,,N,Bayesian-modelled MC-ICP-MS ages
## age_calkaBP_rescale	age,,,calendar kiloyear before present,,speleothems,resampled,,N,rescaled to Bayesian-modelled chronology
## d18OcarbVPDB_rescale	delta 18O,calcium carbonate,,per mil VPDB,,speleothems,,isotope ratio mass spectrometry,N,rescaled to its Bayesian-modelled chronology
#--------------------
